steps.title: Turn the information into two points
steps.content: This is a quick Desmos table/regression problem. The point $(0,-19)$ is already on the line. A slope of $-2$ means that when $x$ increases by $1$, $y$ decreases by $2$, so another point is $(1,-21)$.

steps.title: Fit the line in Desmos
steps.content: In Desmos, run the linear regression $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. It returns $m=-2$ and $b=-19$, so the fitted equation is $y=-2x-19$.

steps.title: Match the fitted equation
steps.content: Choice A has slope $-19$, not $-2$. Choice B has the right slope but goes through $(0,0)$ instead of $(0,-19)$. Choice D has positive slope. Only choice C, $f(x)=-2x-19$, matches the regression result and the original point. Choice C is correct.
